finding: A task-scoped governed commit auto-staged its own generated protected-override audit record, but the active direction lock did not automatically include that record. Pre-commit then failed with `ATM_TASK_DIRECTION_SCOPE_DRIFT` even though every source file was in scope.
expectedBehavior: When ATM generates a task-attached protected audit artifact, task bundle resolution should include it in the direction lock or omit it until the governed closure lane owns it.
evidenceOrRepro: TASK-TEAM-0076 commit attempt was blocked after a clean bundle dry-run; the only blocker was `.atm/history/protected-override-audit/2026-07-12T01-45-43-529Z-POA-c5ec9263d851.json`.
followUp: Fixed 2026-07-14: `listTaskOwnedProtectedOverrideAuditFiles` now feeds `resolveTaskDeclaredScope`, taskflow close bundles, and pre-commit `collectTaskGovernedCommitAllowedFiles`; regression `tests/cli/protected-override-audit-direction-lock.test.ts`.
